title: `drcmd`: Doubly-Robust Causal Inference with Missing Data
text: illustrate use drcmd package, consider missing data problem outcome interest YY missing random conditional measured covariates. assume cheap, noisy proxy measurement YY, denoted Y*Y^*, available subjects predictive missingness (necessary satisfy MAR assumption), resulting following simple data generating process: X∼N(0,1)(Covariates)|X∼Bernoulli(p=logit−1(X))(Treatment)Y|,X∼N(μ=+X+AX,σ2=1)(Outcome)Y*=Y+ε,ε∼N(μ=0,σ2=1/4)(Outcome Proxy)R∼Bernoulli(logit−1(X))(Complete case Indicator) \begin{aligned} X & \sim N(0,1) \ \ \ \ \ \  &\text{(Covariates)} \\ |X & \sim \text{Bernoulli}(p = \text{logit}^{-1}(X)) \ \ \ \ \ \  &\text{(Treatment)} \\ Y|,X & \sim N(\mu=+ X + AX, \sigma^2=1) \ \ \ \ \ \  &\text{(Outcome)} \\ Y^* & = Y + \varepsilon, \ \varepsilon \sim N(\mu=0,\sigma^2 = 1/4) \ \ \ \ \ \  &\text{(Outcome Proxy)} \\ R &\sim \text{Bernoulli}(\text{logit}^{-1}(X)) \ \ \ \ \ \  &\text{(Complete case Indicator)} \\ \end{aligned} YY available complete case indicator R=1R=1, missing random assumption implies Y⟂R|XY \perp R | X. simulate data model : main function drcmd package drcmd(). core arguments Y, X, representing outcome, binary treatment covariates. Users can optionally specify proxy variables W () predictive missing variables, (ii) possibly influence missingness mechanism, (iii) wouldn’t involved causal analysis presence complete data. variables commonly arise semi-supervised inference, cheap proxies often available expensive--measure variables. running example, Y*=WY^*=W. practice, WW can multi-dimensional multiple proxies available. W defaults NA specified user, consistent settings proxies available. Missing data allowed outcome, treatment, covariates (including subset covariates), well combination three. requirement running drcmd exists least one variable never missing, either Y, , X, W. drcmd detects missingness patterns data automatically creates variable R, R=1 observation complete-case R=0 observation missing. Note guarantee identifiability causal estimands 𝔼[Y(1)]\mathbb{E}[Y(1)] 𝔼[Y(0)]\mathbb{E}[Y(0)]. validity resulting estimates hinges MAR assumption holding, crucial problem-specific determination. Along specifying variables, users must specify means estimate nuisance functions. nuisance functions estimated Super Learner (stacking algorithm) using SuperLearner package. 4 nuisance functions fit drcmd, =0,1a = 0,1: 1.ma(X)=𝔼(Y|=,X)2.ga(X)=ℙ(=|X)3.r(Z)=ℙ(R=1|Z)4.φa(Z)=𝔼(χa(X,,Y)|Z,R=1), \begin{aligned} &1. \  m_a(X) = \mathbb{E}(Y|=,X) \\ &2. \ g_a(X) = \mathbb{P}(=|X) \\ &3. \ r(Z)  = \mathbb{P}(R=1|Z) \\ &4. \ \varphi_a(Z) = \mathbb{E}(\chi_a(X,,Y) | Z, R=1), \end{aligned} χa(X)=ma(X)+(=)ga(X)(Y−ma(X))\chi_a(X) = m_a(X) + \frac{(=)}{g_a(X)}(Y-m_a(X)) pseudo-outcome formed efficient influence function estimating counterfactual mean functional 𝔼[𝔼(Y|=,X)]\mathbb{E}[\mathbb{E}(Y|=,X)], ZZ collects variables never subject missingness (always available, regardless whether R=1R=1 R=0R=0). drcmd automatically determines variables comprising ZZ. Users can specify learners nuisance function nuisance-specific arguments , set common library default_learners. nuisance-specific argument overrides default_learners function. argument takes vector SuperLearner library names, using syntax one passes directly SuperLearner. see base libraries available, users can run get_sl_libraries(): list includes SL.hal9001, wrapper highly-adaptive LASSO (HAL). Users can additionally create custom libraries, encouraged consult SuperLearner package documentation details.
code: n <- 1e3 X <- rnorm(n) ; A <- rbinom(n,1,plogis(X)) ; Y <- rnorm(n) + A + X + A*X Ystar <- Y + rnorm(n)/2 ; R <- rbinom(n,1,plogis(X)) ; X <- as.data.frame(X) Y[R==0] <- NA # Make Y NA if R==0  df <- data.frame(Y=Y,A=A,X=X,Ystar=Ystar,R=R) head(df) ##           Y A            X      Ystar R ## 1        NA 0 -1.400043517 -1.7855709 0 ## 2        NA 0  0.255317055 -0.3332133 0 ## 3        NA 0 -2.437263611 -4.6215099 0 ## 4 -1.539838 0 -0.005571287 -2.6864287 1 ## 5        NA 0  0.621552721  0.4344233 0 ## 6        NA 1  1.148411606  4.5822774 0 drcmd::get_sl_libraries() ##  [1] "SL.bartMachine"      "SL.bayesglm"         "SL.biglasso"         ##  [4] "SL.caret"            "SL.caret.rpart"      "SL.cforest"          ##  [7] "SL.earth"            "SL.gam"              "SL.gbm"              ## [10] "SL.glm"              "SL.glm.interaction"  "SL.glmnet"           ## [13] "SL.ipredbagg"        "SL.kernelKnn"        "SL.knn"              ## [16] "SL.ksvm"             "SL.lda"              "SL.leekasso"         ## [19] "SL.lm"               "SL.loess"            "SL.logreg"           ## [22] "SL.mean"             "SL.nnet"             "SL.nnls"             ## [25] "SL.polymars"         "SL.qda"              "SL.randomForest"     ## [28] "SL.ranger"           "SL.ridge"            "SL.rpart"            ## [31] "SL.rpartPrune"       "SL.speedglm"         "SL.speedlm"          ## [34] "SL.step"             "SL.step.forward"     "SL.step.interaction" ## [37] "SL.stepAIC"          "SL.svm"              "SL.template"         ## [40] "SL.xgboost"          "SL.hal9001"

title: `drcmd`: Doubly-Robust Causal Inference with Missing Data
text: Users can view summary estimation procedure calling summary() function, provides point estimates, standard errors 95% CIs main causal estimands. default, drcmd obtains estimates 𝔼[Y(1)]\mathbb{E}[Y(1)], 𝔼[Y(0)]\mathbb{E}[Y(0)], average treatment effect (ATE) 𝔼[Y(1)−Y(0)]\mathbb{E}[Y(1)-Y(0)]. outcome binary, drcmd additionally reports causal risk ratio 𝔼[Y(1)]/𝔼[Y(0)]\mathbb{E}[Y(1)]/\mathbb{E}[Y(0)] odds ratio. Standard errors estimands obtained via delta method (see Technical Details section).

title: `drcmd`: Doubly-Robust Causal Inference with Missing Data
text: running drcmd(), numerous objects stored within resulting output, including results: list containing () parameter estimates stored dataframe named estimates, (ii) standard errors stored dataframe named ses, (iii) nuisance function estimates stored dataframe named nuis params: list containing parameter values used drcmd() R: Binary complete case indicator, 1 denotes complete case U: Names variables partially missing values Z: Names variables missing values Users can obtain detailed summary specifying detail=TRUE summary function:
code: summary(res,detail=TRUE) ## ====================================================================== ##                         Summary of drcmd results                       ## ====================================================================== ## Estimand            Estimate           SE                     95% CI ## ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ## ATE:                   1.098        0.119             [0.864, 1.331] ## E[Y(1)]:               1.116        0.103             [0.915, 1.317] ## E[Y(0)]:               0.018        0.090            [-0.158, 0.194] ## ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Variables with missingness (U): Y ## Variables without missingness (Z): X, A ## ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Validity of results requires causal assumptions to hold, ## as well as the assumption that U is independent of R given Z ## ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ## Number of cross-fitting folds (k): 1  ## Outcome regression nuisance learners: SL.glm SL.gam  ## Propensity score nuisance learners: SL.glm SL.gam  ## Missingness nuisance learners: SL.glm SL.gam  ## Pseudo-outcome nuisance learners: SL.glm SL.gam  ## Estimation method: augmented complete case one-step

title: `drcmd`: Doubly-Robust Causal Inference with Missing Data
text: enabled default, users can estimate parameters cross-fitting setting k argument desired number folds. default, drcmd uses single fold. Cross-fitting encouraged user specifies nuisance learners cover complex function classes, random forests. See technical details section information rationale behind implementation cross-fitting. k > 1, folds independent can fit concurrently setting parallel=TRUE, distributes across cores via parallel::mclapply (supported Windows). Separately, cv_folds argument controls number cross-validation folds SuperLearner uses internally model selection within fit (default 5); lowering speeds estimation cost learner selection.
code: res <- drcmd::drcmd(Y=Y, A=A, X=X,              default_learners='SL.glm',              k=3)

title: `drcmd`: Doubly-Robust Causal Inference with Missing Data
text: Extreme estimated propensity scores, used form inverse probability weights account treatment mechanism missing data mechanism, can lead unstable estimators. mitigate instability, drcmd truncates propensity scores values 0.025 0.975 default. Users can adjust values cutoff argument, truncate propensity scores values cutoff 1-cutoff. instance, avoid truncating weights one can set cutoff=0:
code: res <- drcmd::drcmd(Y=Y, A=A, X=X,              default_learners='SL.glm',              cutoff=0)

what: Doubly-Robust Causal Inference with Missing Data
title: Doubly-Robust Causal Inference with Missing Data
text: Doubly Robust Causal Inference Missing Data Authors: Keith Barnatchez Griffin DesRoches drcmd R package implementing doubly-robust estimators counterfactual means presence general missing data patterns. drcmd leverages links influence curves counterfactual means missingness, influence curve corresponding missingness pattern user-supplied data. Detailed discussion theoretical details behind methods used drcmd can found Kennedy (2016), Tsiatis (2006), van der Laan Robins (2003). Users can fit nuisance functions Super Learner (stacking algorithm). Please see package vignette documentation details. Full documentation available package website.

title: Doubly-Robust Causal Inference with Missing Data
code: devtools::install_github('keithbarnatchez/drcmd')

title: Doubly-Robust Causal Inference with Missing Data
code: # Params for functions eem_ind <- FALSE # TRUE = fit pseudo-outcome regression with empirical efficiency maximiztion default_learners <- c('SL.glm','SL.gam') # default learners used for nuisance functions  k <- 1 # number of cross-fitting folds #------------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Simulate simple missing outcome data structure n <- 1e3 X <- rnorm(n) ; A <- rbinom(n,1,plogis(X)) ; Y <- rnorm(n) + A + X Ystar <- Y + rnorm(n)/2 ; R <- rbinom(n,1,plogis(X)) ; X <- as.data.frame(X)  # Make Y NA if R==0 Y[R==0] <- NA  drcmd_res <- drcmd::drcmd(Y,A,X,  default_learners=default_learners, eem_ind=eem_ind,k=k)

what: Doubly-robust causal inference with missing data — drcmd
title: Doubly-robust causal inference with missing data — drcmd
text: Doubly-robust estimation counterfactual means presence missing data. drcmd() function estimates counterfactual means binary point treatments reports average treatment effects, well causal risk ratios odds ratios binary outcomes. General missingness patterns data allowed automatically determined function – requirement missingness occurs random conditional variables always available. scenarios non-missingness probabilities known, common two-phase sampling designs, users can provide non-missingness probabilities Rprobs argument. Users can fit nuisance functions either highly-adaptive LASSO (HAL) SuperLearner, latter user must specify libraries.

title: Doubly-robust causal inference with missing data — drcmd
code: drcmd(   Y,   A,   X,   W = NA,   R = NA,   default_learners = NULL,   m_learners = NULL,   g_learners = NULL,   r_learners = NULL,   po_learners = NULL,   eem_ind = FALSE,   tml = FALSE,   Rprobs = NA,   k = 1,   cutoff = 0.025,   quiet = TRUE,   cv_folds = 5,   parallel = FALSE,   att = FALSE,   atc = FALSE )

title: Doubly-robust causal inference with missing data — drcmd
text: Y Outcome variable. Can continuous binary binary treatment variable (1=treated, 0=control) X Dataframe containing baseline covariates W (optional) Dataframe containing variables solely predictive missingness, cause outcome exposure. R (optional) character string specifying missingness indicator, 0 indicates missing data. specified, function create missingness indicator identifying missingness pattern data default_learners character vector containing SuperLearner libraries use estimating nuisance functions. User can alternatively specify libraries nuisance function added flexibility m_learners character vector containing learners used outcome regression. vector SuperLearner library names g_learners character vector containing learners used propensity score. vector SuperLearner library names r_learners character vector containing learners used missingness indicator regression. vector SuperLearner library names po_learners character vector containing learners used pseudo-outcome regression. vector SuperLearner library names eem_ind logical indicating whether use empirical efficiency maximization tml logical indicating whether obtain estimates targeted maximum likelihood estimation (TML). TRUE, estimates obtained TML. FALSE (default setting), estimates obtained one-step estimator Rprobs vector probabilities missingness indicator. suitable study designs researcher controls mechanism variables missing (e.g. two-phase sample designs). Defaults NA, case missingness probabilities estimated. k numeric indicating number folds cross-fitting cutoff Cutoff treatment complete case propensity scores. Estimates outside [cutoff, 1-cutoff] set cutoff 1-cutoff, respectively quiet Logical indicating whether suppress progress messages. Default TRUE. Set FALSE see nuisance function estimated. cv_folds Number cross-validation folds used internally SuperLearner model selection. Default 5. Lower values speed estimation. parallel Logical indicating whether run cross-fitting folds parallel using parallel::mclapply. used k > 1. Note: supported Windows. att Logical indicating whether additionally estimate average treatment effect treated (ATT). Default FALSE. atc Logical indicating whether additionally estimate average treatment effect controls (ATC). Default FALSE.

title: Doubly-robust causal inference with missing data — drcmd
code: set.seed(1) n <- 200 X <- rnorm(n) A <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(X)) Y <- rnorm(n) + A + X R_ind <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(X)) Y[R_ind == 0] <- NA covariates <- data.frame(X = X)  fit <- drcmd(Y, A, covariates,              default_learners = "SL.glm",              k = 1) #> Loading required package: nnls fit #> drcmd results #> ------------------------------------------------- #> ATE estimate:  0.9474179  #> ------------------------------------------------- #> Variables with missingness (U):  Y  #> ------------------------------------------------- #> Variables without missingness (Z):  X A  #> ------------------------------------------------- #> Validity of results requires causal assumptions to hold #> As well as the assumption that U is independent of R given Z #> Number of cross-fitting folds (k): 1
